name: EGC
description: Local MCP runtime that gives persistent cross-session memory to 12 AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Amp, and more). SQLite-backed state survives context resets. Install: npm install -g @egchq/egc

tools.name: get_state
tools.description: Returns the current project memory: decisions made, preferences established, things to avoid, and what to pick up next. Call this at the START of every session to restore context.

tools.name: update_state
tools.description: Updates the project memory with decisions made this session. Call this at the END of every session. Merges with existing state — does not erase previous memory.

tools.name: store_decision
tools.description: Store a decision with lock arbitration. Records a significant decision with its context for future reference.

tools.name: query_history
tools.description: Query past decisions. Returns a paginated list of decisions stored in the session memory.

tools.name: search_history
tools.description: Keyword search over the decision history with BM25 relevance ranking (SQLite FTS5). Returns decisions ordered by relevance with normalized scores.

tools.name: get_project_state
tools.description: Get current state for the active project. Returns server status metadata including storage engine and arbitration mode.

tools.name: validate_command
tools.description: Validate command execution safety. Checks whether a shell command is safe to run before execution.

tools.name: validate_write
tools.description: Validate write path safety. Checks whether a file path is safe to write before creating or modifying files.

tools.name: reduce_context
tools.description: Adaptive Context Routing: Loads files and mutates payloads to save IDE token budget. Reduces context window usage by intelligently compressing file payloads.

tools.name: orchestrate_task
tools.description: Routes a prompt with optional agent/skill lists and file payloads. Returns routing context and context-reduction metrics. Agent/skill selection is pass-through; provide explicit lists for deterministic routing.
